author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
In which the remaining judgments of the Council of Carthage are examined.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

originalBook: In which the remaining judgments of the Council of Carthage are examined.
title: Chapter 12

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.text: Pomponius of Dionysiana said: "It is manifest that heretics cannot baptize and give remission of sins, seeing that no power is given to them that they should be able either to loose or bind anything on earth."
The answer is: This power is not given to murderers either, that is, to those who hate their brothers.
For it was not said to such as these, "whosesoever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whosesoever sins ye retain, they are retained."
And yet they baptize, and both Paul tolerates them in the same communion of baptism, and Cyprian acknowledges them.
